evals.prompt: Can an autonomous loop improve a training recipe overnight?
evals.expected_output: A fixed-harness research program that freezes the evaluator, data split, time budget, prompts, and dependencies before any candidate loop runs.
expectations: States a falsifiable Claim with domain, baseline, candidate family, primary metric, guardrails, hidden costs, and win condition.
Defines the Verifier Boundary by naming frozen evaluator, frozen data split, fixed time budget, immutable prompts/dependencies, mutable recipe candidate, and tampering rules.
Defines Baseline / Candidate Family and a keep/reset frontier rule for candidate recipes.
Lists Enemy Terms including evaluator-hacking, hidden-cost-leak, data leakage, extra supervision, failed-run hiding, runtime, and dependency changes.
Includes Rejection Gates that reject modifying evaluator, changing data split, extending time budget, changing prompts/dependencies, or hiding failed runs.
Includes a Proof Ladder / Transfer Path with local fixture score, held-out split, and production-style transfer check.
Includes a Proof Ledger Decision using CONTINUE, REFINE, PIVOT, or REJECT.
Names a concrete Next Pressure test targeting evaluator-hacking or no-transfer-gate.

evals.prompt: Can a new compression representation beat the current baseline?
evals.expected_output: A compression proof program that defines the input family, baseline representation, cost accounting, rejection gates, and shortest losing fixture.
expectations: States a falsifiable Claim with input family, baseline codec or representation, candidate family, measured output quality, required delta, and guardrails.
Defines the Verifier Boundary by freezing corpus split, evaluator, quality metric, metadata accounting, decode environment, and candidate-visible data.
Defines Baseline / Candidate Family and the exact candidate representation space.
Lists Enemy Terms including hidden-cost-leak, no-transfer-gate, metadata size, decode cost, complexity, fallback path, distribution shift, and status-inflation risk.
Includes Rejection Gates that reject generic 'smaller' claims, ignored metadata, ignored decode cost, or testing only training data.
Includes a Proof Ladder / Transfer Path from toy examples to held-out data and a production-style decode constraint.
Includes a Proof Ledger Decision using CONTINUE, REFINE, PIVOT, or REJECT.
Names a concrete Next Pressure test: the shortest fixture that makes the representation lose.

evals.prompt: Can multiple agents discover a better alignment method if we keep whatever scores best?
evals.expected_output: A divergent-researcher protocol that keeps scoring frozen, treats evaluation as the bottleneck, and rejects reward-hacked winners.
expectations: States a falsifiable Claim with baseline method, candidate method family, shared score, guardrails, human review budget, and transfer condition.
Defines the Verifier Boundary by freezing the scoring function, hidden evals, selection rule, reviewer rubric, and what agents may not inspect or change.
Defines Baseline / Candidate Family with diverse starting priors and a shared comparison protocol.
Lists Enemy Terms including evaluator-hacking, reward hacking, hidden-cost-leak, human review burden, supervision cost, generic-pressure, and single benchmark overfit.
Includes Rejection Gates that reject 'keep whatever scores best' unless the score resists gaming and transfer checks pass.
Includes a Proof Ladder / Transfer Path from candidate construction to held-out domains and adversarial review.
Includes a Proof Ledger Decision using CONTINUE, REFINE, PIVOT, or REJECT.
Names a concrete Next Pressure test targeting evaluator-hacking or reward-hacked selection.

evals.prompt: Can LLM-generated programs discover a better bin-packing heuristic?
evals.expected_output: An evaluator-gated program-search design that separates correctness, complexity, empirical runtime, and distribution transfer.
expectations: States a falsifiable Claim with problem class, baseline heuristic, generated-program candidate family, metric, guardrails, and win condition.
Defines the Verifier Boundary by freezing correctness tests, benchmark distribution, runtime environment, complexity accounting, and evaluator access.
Defines Baseline / Candidate Family and stores frontier programs with scores and complexity notes.
Lists Enemy Terms including evaluator-hacking, proof-gap, no-transfer-gate, skipped correctness, hidden asymptotic cost, and benchmark overfit.
Includes Rejection Gates that reject candidates that skip correctness, exploit evaluator weaknesses, hide asymptotic cost, or overfit benchmark distribution.
Includes a Proof Ladder / Transfer Path separating examples, counterexample search, correctness proof/checker, empirical runtime, and held-out instances.
Includes a Proof Ledger Decision using CONTINUE, REFINE, PIVOT, or REJECT.
Names a concrete Next Pressure test targeting evaluator-hacking in the generated program.

evals.prompt: Can we synthesize several modern AI research workflows into one reusable method?
evals.expected_output: An evidence-synthesis review that separates source facts, author interpretation, and inference while producing a method matrix with tradeoffs.
expectations: States a falsifiable Claim defining the reusable-method scope, baseline workflow, candidate synthesis, success criteria, and transfer condition.
Defines the Verifier Boundary by naming current-source checks, accepted source types, mutable synthesis, review process, and source-claim limits.
Defines Baseline / Candidate Family with existing methods as baselines and candidate synthesis patterns as the allowed family.
Lists Enemy Terms including source-confusion, status-inflation, generic-pressure, stale sources, cherry-picked evidence, and treating narratives as proof.
Includes Rejection Gates that reject stale sources, organization-authored narratives treated as neutral proof, and missing method tradeoffs.
Includes a Proof Ladder / Transfer Path from source facts to method matrix, counter-sources, and transfer to a held-out research workflow.
Includes a Proof Ledger Decision using CONTINUE, REFINE, PIVOT, or REJECT.
Names a concrete Next Pressure test that could weaken the favorite synthesis.

evals.prompt: Can we prove this greedy algorithm is optimal for every instance in this problem class?
evals.expected_output: A proof-oriented research program with definitions, quantifiers, counterexample search, proof method, lemmas, rejection gates, status label, and next pressure.
expectations: States a falsifiable Claim with precise objects, assumptions, quantifiers, baseline algorithm, greedy candidate, and scope of optimality.
Defines the Verifier Boundary by naming acceptable proof/checker, mutable proof attempt, forbidden quantifier changes, and reviewer criteria.
Defines Baseline / Candidate Family with the greedy algorithm and competing constructions or known algorithms.
Lists Enemy Terms including proof-gap, soft-claim, status-inflation, examples-as-proof, missing quantifiers, and skipped counterexample search.
Includes Rejection Gates that reject PROVEN status unless the proof covers every instance in the stated class.
Includes a Proof Ladder / Transfer Path with examples, counterexample search, invariants, lemmas, proof sketch, executable checker, and formal or expert verification.
Includes a Proof Ledger Decision using CONTINUE, REFINE, PIVOT, or REJECT.
Names a concrete Next Pressure test: the smallest counterexample search space or edge case family.

evals.prompt: Does intervention X improve outcome Y in population Z?
evals.expected_output: A scientific hypothesis plan with controls, confounders, measurement method, exclusion criteria, replication, and causality guardrails.
expectations: States a falsifiable Claim with intervention, outcome, population, baseline/control, effect size, measurement method, and win condition.
Defines the Verifier Boundary by freezing study design, measurement instrument, inclusion/exclusion criteria, analysis plan, and what cannot change after observing data.
Defines Baseline / Candidate Family with control condition and allowed intervention variants.
Lists Enemy Terms including soft-claim, hidden-cost-leak, no-transfer-gate, confounders, measurement bias, correlation-as-causation, and replication risk.
Includes Rejection Gates that reject claims without controls, falsifiable measurement, confounder handling, or replication/held-out validation.
Includes a Proof Ladder / Transfer Path from pilot measurement to controlled study, replication, and held-out population validation.
Includes a Proof Ledger Decision using CONTINUE, REFINE, PIVOT, or REJECT.
Names a concrete Next Pressure test targeting the most likely confounder or measurement failure.

evals.prompt: Will this event-driven architecture reduce coupling without increasing operational risk?
evals.expected_output: An architecture claim review with coupling metric, baseline architecture, candidate architecture, operational guardrails, rollback gate, and production transfer.
expectations: States a falsifiable Claim with coupling metric, baseline architecture, event-driven candidate, risk guardrails, hidden costs, and win condition.
Defines the Verifier Boundary by freezing architecture comparison criteria, operational risk metrics, observability requirements, rollback criteria, and migration scope.
Defines Baseline / Candidate Family with current architecture and allowed event-driven changes.
Lists Enemy Terms including generic-pressure, hidden-cost-leak, no-transfer-gate, migration cost, observability gaps, failure modes, latency, and team cognition.
Includes Rejection Gates that reject architecture taste as evidence, ignored migration cost, missing rollback, or untested production-style constraints.
Includes a Proof Ladder / Transfer Path from dependency map to fixture migration, failure injection, rollback drill, and production transfer gate.
Includes a Proof Ledger Decision using CONTINUE, REFINE, PIVOT, or REJECT.
Names a concrete Next Pressure test targeting operational risk or rollback failure.

evals.prompt: Can this benchmark prove our model is better?
evals.expected_output: An evaluation-validity analysis that separates benchmark validity from model performance, charges contamination/saturation/grading risk, and prevents status inflation.
expectations: States a falsifiable Claim that separates benchmark-validity scope from model-performance superiority.
Defines the Verifier Boundary by freezing benchmark data, grading rubric, contamination checks, model access rules, and evaluator mutability.
Defines Baseline / Candidate Family with compared model(s), baseline model, benchmark scope, and allowed prompt/config changes.
Lists Enemy Terms including status-inflation, source-confusion, evaluator-hacking, contamination, saturation, subjective grading without rubric, and distribution mismatch.
Includes Rejection Gates that reject the word PROVEN unless benchmark scope, contamination, saturation, and grading objectivity support it.
Includes a Proof Ladder / Transfer Path from benchmark score to held-out, adversarial, or production-style evaluation.
Includes a Proof Ledger Decision using CONTINUE, REFINE, PIVOT, or REJECT.
Names a concrete Next Pressure test targeting evaluator-hacking or benchmark contamination.

evals.prompt: Will this onboarding change increase activation?
evals.expected_output: A product-experiment plan with activation metric, baseline, candidate change, sample, guardrails, retention checks, and shipment rejection gates.
expectations: States a falsifiable Claim with activation metric, baseline onboarding, candidate change, sample, guardrails, effect threshold, and win condition.
Defines the Verifier Boundary by freezing experiment design, assignment logic, metric definition, analysis window, segment rules, and shipment criteria.
Defines Baseline / Candidate Family with current onboarding and allowed candidate changes.
Lists Enemy Terms including soft-claim, hidden-cost-leak, no-transfer-gate, novelty effects, segment shift, support burden, and long-term retention.
Includes Rejection Gates that reject shipping from short-term lift only, ignored segment shift, missing retention guardrail, or unsupported causal design.
Includes a Proof Ladder / Transfer Path from A/B or quasi-experiment to segment analysis and retention transfer check.
Includes a Proof Ledger Decision using CONTINUE, REFINE, PIVOT, or REJECT.
Names a concrete Next Pressure test targeting retention decay or segment-shift failure.

evals.prompt: Can an agent keep researching this topic until it finds a strong answer?
evals.expected_output: A falsifiable research loop with explicit done criteria, compact state, observable actions, verifier checks, stop conditions, ledger decision, and next pressure.
expectations: States a falsifiable Claim defining what a strong answer means, baseline research process, candidate agent loop, evidence standard, and win condition.
Defines the Verifier Boundary by naming done criteria, budget, retry limits, stop conditions, compact state, and what the agent cannot change.
Defines Baseline / Candidate Family with manual or fixed-process research as baseline and allowed tool-using agent loop variants.
Lists Enemy Terms including loop-drift, generic-pressure, status-inflation, stale state, repeated failing action, unobservable progress, and acting without observation.
Includes Rejection Gates that reject continuing when observations stop changing the proof ledger, when retry limits are exhausted, or when verifier tampering occurs.
Includes a Proof Ladder / Transfer Path from action observations to verification, ledger updates, and stop/continue decision.
Includes a Proof Ledger Decision using CONTINUE, REFINE, PIVOT, or REJECT.
Names a concrete Next Pressure test targeting loop-drift or repeated failing action.
